summary: Alfred is a well-architected, well-documented Rust editor at M2 of 7 milestones. Clean crate separation with compile-time boundary enforcement, thorough ADR documentation, and solid test coverage for implemented scope. Main gaps: stub crates not yet implemented, no property-based testing, and one hotspot file (app.rs) accumulating complexity.

documentation_coverage.notes: Every public function and type has doc comments. 6 ADRs cover all major decisions. 5 design documents with C4 diagrams, data models, and component boundaries. No README.md at repository root. CLAUDE.md serves as project-level documentation.

architecture_clarity.notes: Crate boundaries enforce layer separation at compile time. No circular dependencies. Consistent module-level free function style throughout alfred-core. Naming follows Rust conventions with clear Given/When/Then test names.

top_hotspots.notes: Largest file. Accumulated event loop, key conversion, key dispatch, command-line mode, and Lisp eval wiring across M1 and M2. Three distinct responsibilities mixed together.
All 7 bridge primitives. Already refactored once (commit 992ac0d) to extract helper functions. Well-structured after refactor.
Lisp runtime wrapper + performance baseline tests (large test module). Stable after M2.
Clean rendering code. Generic over Backend for testability. Will need extension for M4 (gutter) and M5 (status bar).
Pure movement functions with thorough tests. Stable and well-tested.

risks.title: Plugin API sufficiency for modal editing
risks.description: The walking skeleton's thesis depends on M7 (Vim modal editing as a Lisp plugin). If the primitive API is insufficient, the architecture claim fails. M6 and M7 will be the definitive tests.
risks.mitigation: Kill signals defined in roadmap. ADR-004 provides fallback to Janet if performance is the bottleneck.

risks.title: app.rs complexity growth
risks.description: At 861 lines with 3 distinct responsibilities, app.rs is the top hotspot. Each milestone adds to it. M6 will require replacing hardcoded key dispatch with keymap resolution.
risks.mitigation: Extract key_conversion.rs and key_dispatch.rs before M6.

risks.title: rust_lisp maintenance trajectory
risks.description: rust_lisp has ~300 GitHub stars and fewer contributors than Janet. If the project becomes inactive, Alfred depends on it.
risks.mitigation: rust_lisp is simple enough to fork. Migration to Janet is isolated to alfred-lisp crate.

risks.title: Design docs diverge from implementation
risks.description: component-boundaries.md states 'no Rc<RefCell>' but M2 implementation uses it for Lisp bridge sharing.
risks.mitigation: Update design docs to reflect M2 reality.

risks.title: Rc<RefCell> runtime borrow panics
risks.description: RefCell provides runtime borrow checking. Violating borrow rules panics instead of compile error.
risks.mitigation: Confined to event loop boundary. Careful borrow scoping in app.rs (drop before eval). Well-tested paths.

risks.title: Property-based testing not adopted
risks.description: CLAUDE.md prescribes property-based testing as default strategy, but no proptest/quickcheck usage found.
risks.mitigation: Low immediate risk. Can be adopted incrementally for cursor and buffer operations.

recommendations.title: Extract app.rs into focused modules before M6
recommendations.description: Split alfred-tui/src/app.rs into three files: key_conversion.rs (pure crossterm-to-domain mapping), key_dispatch.rs (pure key event handling and command-line mode), and app.rs (event loop I/O only). This will make the M6 keybinding refactor significantly cleaner.

recommendations.title: Update design docs to reflect Rc<RefCell> reality
recommendations.description: component-boundaries.md section 5 states the design avoids Rc<RefCell>. Update to document the M2 decision to use it for Lisp bridge sharing, with rationale (single-threaded execution makes RefCell safe).

recommendations.title: Add property-based tests for cursor and buffer modules
recommendations.description: CLAUDE.md prescribes property-based testing as the default strategy. Buffer operations (insert/delete roundtrip) and cursor movements (ensure_within_bounds is idempotent) are natural candidates for proptest.

recommendations.title: Add README.md to repository root
recommendations.description: The repository has CLAUDE.md but no README.md. A brief README with project description, build instructions, and architecture overview would help new contributors.

recommendations.title: Implement acceptance tests in tests/acceptance/
recommendations.description: The acceptance test files for M1 and M2 exist but all tests are #[ignore] with todo!() bodies. These should be enabled and implemented to serve as regression guards across milestones.

git_analysis.notes: All commits in a 3-day window. M1 and M2 implemented sequentially with clear commit boundaries per feature. Commit messages follow conventional commit format with scope.

score_breakdown.notes: Documentation score: high due to 6 ADRs, inline docs, design docs; small deduction for missing README. Architecture score: high due to clean layers, zero circular deps, consistent naming; small deduction for app.rs accumulation. Hotspot score: moderate due to app.rs being a clear complexity hotspot with growth trajectory.
